features: Reset behavior and default CSR values
CFG mode control: hmac_en, sha_en, endian_swap, digest_swap
CMD W1S sequencing: hash_start, hash_process, hash_stop
STATUS register correctness: fifo_full, fifo_empty, fifo_depth, sha_idle
Message FIFO push/pop behavior and depth tracking
HMAC functional flow with key processing and double-pass operation
Digest register readout and byte-swap behavior
Illegal configuration handling: hmac_en without sha_en
Multi-block streaming across arbitrary chunk sizes

directed_tests.name: reset_and_csr_smoke
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset values, TL-UL read/write access to all implemented CSRs, reserved-bit read-as-zero behavior, and unmapped address error response.

directed_tests.name: cfg_mode_matrix
directed_tests.intent: Exercise valid and invalid CFG combinations across bare SHA and HMAC modes, including endian_swap and digest_swap permutations.

directed_tests.name: sha_single_block_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program bare SHA mode, hash a single 512-bit message block, and compare digest against a golden SHA-256 reference.

directed_tests.name: sha_multi_block_streaming
directed_tests.intent: Write a message in multiple FIFO chunks, issue hash_process across block boundaries, and verify final digest matches the reference model.

directed_tests.name: sha_partial_block_padding
directed_tests.intent: Finalize messages of varying non-block-aligned lengths to validate automatic padding and length encoding.

directed_tests.name: hmac_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Load a known HMAC key and message, run the full HMAC sequence, and compare the final digest against a software reference.

directed_tests.name: hmac_key_zeroization_wipe
directed_tests.intent: After HMAC operation, write WIPE_SECRET and confirm key registers and secret-related state are cleared.

directed_tests.name: fifo_full_empty_depth
directed_tests.intent: Fill the 32-entry FIFO to capacity, verify fifo_full assertion and depth tracking, then drain and verify fifo_empty.

directed_tests.name: cmd_sequence_illegal_order
directed_tests.intent: Attempt hash_process and hash_stop before hash_start, and verify the DUT either ignores or flags the sequence per observed RTL behavior without deadlock.

directed_tests.name: digest_swap_endian_swap
directed_tests.intent: Independently toggle endian_swap and digest_swap to confirm byte-order transformations on input words and digest output words.

random_tests.name: constrained_random_sha_stream
random_tests.intent: Randomize message lengths, chunk sizes, FIFO write pacing, and command timing in bare SHA mode while checking against a Python SHA-256 model.

random_tests.name: constrained_random_hmac_stream
random_tests.intent: Randomize key values, message lengths, and streaming patterns in HMAC mode, including partial blocks and finalization, with golden HMAC comparison.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzzer
random_tests.intent: Randomly issue aligned CSR reads/writes, reserved-bit writes, and unmapped accesses to stress TL-UL protocol handling and register decode.

random_tests.name: status_polling_stress
random_tests.intent: Randomly poll STATUS during active hashing to validate fifo_depth, fifo_empty/full, and sha_idle transitions under concurrent traffic.

risk_areas.reason: The design performs an inner hash followed by an automatic outer hash; bugs here can silently corrupt authentication results and are high impact.
SHA-256 finalization is error-prone for partial blocks and multi-block streams; off-by-one or length encoding defects are likely.
The 32-entry FIFO is central to streaming operation; incorrect backpressure or depth accounting can cause data loss or deadlock.
hmac_en without sha_en is explicitly invalid and may expose undefined internal state or unexpected behavior.
endian_swap and digest_swap affect interoperability and are easy to implement incorrectly, but are localized to data formatting.
Secret clearing must reliably remove key material and internal state; failures are security-relevant but likely limited in scope.
Unmapped access, ready/valid backpressure, and same-cycle response behavior can cause integration issues, though functional scope is narrower.
Only lightly specified in the excerpt; basic sanity is needed, but exhaustive validation is lower priority within the time budget.
